sourceMovie.title: Code Geass: Lelouch of the Re;surrection
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Code Geass: Lelouch of the Re;surrection works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Code Geass: Lelouch of the Re;surrection clicked for you, Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and science fiction, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Code Geass: Lelouch of the Re;surrection?
faq.answer: Try Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone, Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance, Evangelion: 3.0+1.0 Thrice Upon a Time—each shares DNA with Code Geass: Lelouch of the Re;surrection while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Code Geass: Lelouch of the Re;surrection?
faq.answer: Code Geass: Lelouch of the Re;surrection (2019) is best described as Animation, Science Fiction, Crime, Mystery, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Code Geass: Lelouch of the Re;surrection part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Code Geass: Lelouch of the Rebellion Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
